title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Expose Publish Bind Address
summary: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker expose publish bind address increment in least-exposure container network topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Expose Publish Bind Address · Theory Docker Expose Publish Bind Address
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Expose Publish Bind Address contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior. A defensible solution applies Docker Expose Publish Bind Address, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-exposure container network topology.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-expose-publish-bind-address — Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-4
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker networking dns publishing 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: EXPOSE publishes a port to the host and internet. This fails the Docker Expose Publish Bind Address contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
